column59: I got a new job and had to change insurance providers.
column60:
States, "I change jobs a lot so my insurance changes. My old practitioner is no longer with headway, so I had to find someone else and that's what brought me here." 

Reviewed intake forms and client noted that he struggles with excessive hand washing and able to work through the anxiety of not being able to wash hands when out and about. Keeps hand sanitizer on him as coping mechanism/behavior intervention. Client also adds,  "I generally have an obsessive personality and I get really distressed if something isn't up to my standards or go the way I wanted. I like things to be perfect." Client states--"if something is misaligned with expectations, I do obsess over it. An example is mold remediation didn't go the way I wanted it to and I had them redo it. My friends say it's probably fine, but I want to do it right." Will continue to monitor. Endorses some ruminations, but does not interfere with ability to function.

Client states, "I think I've had anxiety my whole life, but my provider at Kaiser states that he thinks it's a bi product of ADHD and things have gotten better with my anxiety since being on ADHD medication." First dx with ADHD in adulthood, about 3 years ago. Took a long intake assessment and ADHD questionnaire. Formally dx with ADHD, though he is unsure which kind. Open to taking DIVA questionnaire before next session and this provider agrees to send in ADHD medication in the interim. 

Currently on Vyvanse 30 mg and finds it both effective, though intolerable-"it's completely find during the day, I just hate the comedown...I get really crabby and I only take it 3 days a week because I don't like the comedown." Finds that the Vyvanse lasts about 10 hours. Trialed Qelbree (non-stimulant) and was intolerable (nausea). States that sleep has not been impacted by the Vyvanse. Appetite  is reduced, though mitigated by being intentional about regular meals. Taking Vyvanse with protein rich breakfast. 

Over the past several weeks, the patient denies sustained sadness. Denies anhedonia, recent changes to appetite, energy level, and ability to concentrate. The patient denies hopelessness, worthlessness, SI/HI/AH/VH/delusions. Denies any discrete periods with decreased need for sleep, euphoria or grandiosity or irritability, flights of idea, impulsivity, uncharacteristic talkativeness or rapid speech. Endorses history of depression x 1in 2016, which lasted 1 year--"triggered by relationship issues. I stopped lifting weights, stopped eating well, and started drinking every day. " States that he was experiencing anhedonia and passive suicidal thoughts--"but then I got too fat and my clothes didn't fit and I realized it was time to turn my life around." Support system family and partner. 

Over the past several weeks, the patient denies excessive worrying. Deniues excessive and hard-to-control worrying, though sometimes ruminates about interpersonal conflict. Denies Feeling keyed up and on edge, difficulty concentration or mind going blank, irritability, and muscle tension. Denies panic attacks. The patient denies hopelessness, worthlessness, SI/HI/AH/VH/delusions.

When taking ADHD medication, the patient reports improved symptoms:
- the patient is giving closer attention to details and does not complain of making mistakes at work or missing deadlines.
- the patient is better able to sustain attention during tasks and conversations.
- the patient is better able to quell mind wandering and actually listens when spoken to directly.
- the patient follows through on instructions and duties in the workplace and is not easily side tracked.
- the patient has less difficulty organizing tasks and activities (eg, has better time management; is able to meet deadlines).
- the patient does not complain of avoiding tasks that require sustained mental effort (eg, preparing reports, completing forms, reviewing lengthy papers).
- the patient does not complain of misplacing things necessary for tasks or activities (eg, tools, wallets, keys, paperwork, eyeglasses, mobile telephones).
- the patient does not complain he is easily distracted by extraneous stimuli (eg, unrelated thoughts).
- the patient is less forgetful in daily activities (eg, doing chores, running errands; for older adolescents and adults, returning calls, paying bills, keeping appointments).
- the patient denies problems with fidgeting, being often "on the go," acting as if "driven by a motor" (eg, is unable to be or uncomfortable being still for extended time, as in restaurants, meetings), talking excessively, having a hard time waiting in line, often interrupting or intruding on others.

Denies any discrete periods with decreased need for sleep, euphoria or grandiosity or irritability, flights of idea, impulsivity, uncharacteristic talkativeness or rapid speech.

Denies si/hi/avh/pi. Denies mania.

column60:
States, "I have been pretty good. I've had the second work from home day now for two weeks and I just feel so much more productive, get so much done. I used to struggle with snoozing my alarm and stuff and take action to start my day. But I feel it's better now that I'm working from home for 2 days. I don't feel as worn out." She is now wfh on Wednesday and Fridays. 

The past few weeks she has also only been needing 1 tab of Trazodone for sleep. Tolerating well, notes efficacy. 

Tolerating Zoloft 100 mg daily, "I feel good, a lot better than on the 75 mg. It's a lot better. column60:
Client presents to appointment on time. Began Lexapro as prescribed, tolerating well. No efficacy at this time, though this is to be expected given inadequate trial. 

Also trialed hydroxyzine prn or sleep last week x 1 night; was helpful for sleep, though experienced some dizziness the next day, which she attributes to hydroxyzine. Elects to discontinue this medication and would like to trial alternative for sleep. Has not yet trialed the propranolol. 

Discussed Seroquel 50 mg; 1 full tablet po qhs for sleep. Verbalizes understanding of indications for medication and directions. R/b/se discussed, elects to proceed. 

Will increase Lexparo to 10 mg today, taking at lunchtime. 

Denies any discrete periods with decreased need for sleep, euphoria or grandiosity or irritability, flights of idea, impulsivity, uncharacteristic talkativeness or rapid speech. 

She states that she was experiencing hair falling out, fast heart rate/panic attacks while at work. Endured panic attack x 1 in July during meeting. She tried to return to work after this, but kept crying at desk while at work and decided to take a leave of absence.  Sought therapy x 1 session, though felt it was not a good fit, nor supportive enough for her at this time. 

No panic attacks since last session. 

She withdrew her application for promotion due to her anxiety and intense stress she was under, recognizing that her health was being impacted by this. Easily ruminates over work challenges, especially when mind is not occupied--crying while driving, doing the dishes, and other mundane tasks. Feels her distress tolerance is low, easily rattled. She goes out of her way to avoid driving past work, which is close to her home.

No improvement in the above symptoms. 

Over the past several weeks, the patient endorses sustained sadness, crying. Endorses feelings of powerlessness, loss of agency and competency, isolation, loss of interest ("my job was my passion, but I couldn't look at gems anymore"). Endorses low energy, difficulty concentrating. 

Endorses persistent hopelessness, worthlessness, "I was top of my class in my home Country, but now I don't feel like I know anything. I feel like my supervisors are not being transparent, so how can I do anything to help the situation?" 

Support system consists of husband, who is very "patient" with her, and upset on her behalf. Children are 16 (son) and 12 (daughter) yoa. 

Denies suicidal ideation, "no, never." Denies self injurious behaviors. Denies HI/AH/VH/delusions. 

Completes ADLs without significant effort, though she feels that she has cared less about her appearance since last November, "I don't dye my hair anymore." 

Primary pain point: "Nothing makes me happy now, I'd be happier."

Background: 
Shiva is a 45 year old female patient who presents as very tearful, alluding to upsetting event at work. She is a gemologist and has PHD, immigrant from Pakistan. She got her dream job at GIA and uprooted family from Tucson to Carlsbad. She was enjoying this job for 2 years. However, when it came time from promotion, despite being in rigorous training  x1 year to promote, she did not receive the promotion she felt she deserved. She felt that her supervisors did not have reason for not promoting her, despite her asking for concrete feedback. Her dissatisfaction re: promotion was sent up the chain of command and she was told to re-start this stressful training program all over. She feels very stuck and upset with her circumstances, feeling that she is in unjust circumstances, with perceived lack of transparency about her performance causing significant anxiety, This has been ongoing since April 2024, with mental health symptoms related to this scenario occurring in November 2024, and coming to a head in July 2025.

"I've always had issues with time management, procrastination, and focusing on one task at at time. I've also always been impulsive- making very extensive plans and then having to cancel when the time comes bc I don't really want to do the activity, shopping and having to return things bc I don't actually want them when they arrive, signing up for things and not really wanting to attend when they come up...everything sounds good at the time and I mean well. I'm always late for things and rushing - it's so stressful. Now that I'm older, I don't like the stress. The procrastination has been forever. I was always the person going to sleep at night and waking up at 4 AM to do homework assignments. Again, stressful. Now, I have so many things to do, it's not great letting them pile up. I run an  animal rescue and have two jobs, I already have so much stress so this way of living is not ideal. I also am so sick of losing things! CONSTANTLY, like everyday I lose my phone, keys, or wallet. I don't know if it's part of this, but it's annoying. (I now have little sensors on everything so I can ping them to find them) I walk from one place to another and get interrupted bc of the animals or so much to clean and start doing something else when I was already working on something in the office. Anyway, things get done, I'm just tired of rushing all the time because I don't do things in order or complete tasks before moving on to the next. I don't know. I try to set alarms, but I just let them go off thinking I have a timer in my head and it's fine for a few minutes- then I'm still late. I make daily lists - many of them - but never complete them. My therapist mentioned meds could potentially help. I don't know. It's been this way my whole life.

Anxiety - ever since I had covid I have suffered debilitating anxiety.  it got worse as more time went on. Tried Sertraline (Zoloft generic) 50mg daily since July 17, 2025.  I had successfully used Zoloft for about 5 months when going through my divorce in 1996, so Emily thought we'd try it again.  it has helped dramatically.  For example, I used to have to plan/psych myself up to go to the grocery store and sometimes couldn't overcome the anxiety and didn't go.  Now, taking the Sertraline, I don't have to psych myself up, it's a simple thing to go to the grocery store now.  I have gone to the grocery store, clothes shopping, Costco, driving, attended a musical, and even had a small group of friends over for a day by the pool and BBQ.  It's definitely been a game changer for me.

column60:
Assuming care after maternity leave from NP Emily Samuel. When this provider was gone, EKG x was abnormal, though eccocardiogram showed healthy and normal cardiac function. Primary care physician medically cleared client for stimulant medication. Has been on Adderall steadily since. Client also discontinued abilify while this provider was on maternity leave (states that he gained too much weight). 

Client also shares that he discontinued Lexapro about 1.5 weeks ago, "it's better to feel something than numb." Since discontinuing the Lexapro, has experienced some reemergence of depressive symptoms--"more down than usual, some passive suicidal thoughts, but no specific plans or anything. Nothing serious for me." States that depressive symptoms are better during the day, however, mood tends to worsen in the evenings. Note that the client discontinued the Lexapro 20 mg cold turkey, did not wean off of this. Encouraged to wean off together, from 20 mg, to 15 mg, to 10 mg, to 5 mg and d/c, though client declines. Client states that depression at this time is manageable and wishes to remain off of Lexapro despite reemergence of depressive symptoms. Client states, "I do not feel I improved much anyway from Lexapro or Abilify. I have been feeling down for the majority of my life, maybe it's just the way I am. Nothing I can't manage." 

We discussed retrying Wellbutrin to target mood instead, as client endorses great benefit in mood from the ADHD medications. Client wishes to remain off of Lexapro for an adequate period of "washout." Client verbalizes ability to reach out to this provider if needs support from antidepressant earlier and we reviewed safety plan. 

Client recently moved to Brentwood with family and attributes some of his sadness to having moved to a new space--"a small house in the middle of nowhere." Also states that he is getting used to driving, which he didn't have to do when he was living in San Francisco. Client endorses fear surrounding driving. Client is also looking for a new job, as he does not like his current employment. 

Client remains over all isolative. Using 3D printer for projects, which he enjoys. 

He states that his sleep schedule has much improved, going to sleep around midnight, and waking 0730/0800. 

Endorses depression in the evening--"but nothing I can't shake. Distraction helps. It's nothing new." States that he has experienced dysthymia over the past 10 years. We discussed ketamine treatment in town to address the low mood and client is open to it. Reviewed r/b/se of the same. 

Denies any discrete periods with decreased need for sleep, euphoria or grandiosity or irritability, flights of idea, impulsivity, uncharacteristic talkativeness or rapid speech.

Patient reports compliance to medication, Adderall. Patient reports they are unable to function without their medication. Denies side effects or other adverse reactions to the medication. Reports appetite, sleep, energy, and concentration intact. Denies current si/hi/ah/vh/paranoia. Denies new medical issues since last visit.

When taking ADHD medication, the patient reports improved symptoms:
- the patient is giving closer attention to details and does not complain of making mistakes at work or missing deadlines.
- the patient is better able to sustain attention during tasks and conversations.
- the patient is better able to quell mind wandering and actually listens when spoken to directly.
- the patient follows through on instructions and duties in the workplace and is not easily side tracked.
- the patient has less difficulty organizing tasks and activities (eg, has better time management; is able to meet deadlines).
- the patient does not complain of avoiding tasks that require sustained mental effort (eg, preparing reports, completing forms, reviewing lengthy papers).
- the patient does not complain of misplacing things necessary for tasks or activities (eg, tools, wallets, keys, paperwork, eyeglasses, mobile telephones).
- the patient does not complain he is easily distracted by extraneous stimuli (eg, unrelated thoughts).
- the patient is less forgetful in daily activities (eg, doing chores, running errands; for older adolescents and adults, returning calls, paying bills, keeping appointments).
- the patient denies problems with fidgeting, being often "on the go," acting as if "driven by a motor" (eg, is unable to be or uncomfortable being still for extended time, as in restaurants, meetings), talking excessively, having a hard time waiting in line, often interrupting or intruding on others.

column60:
32 year old male with hx of ptsd, anxiety and a recent traumatic event, presents for f/u. Appointment to assume care from Emily Samuel, NP. 

Reason for seeking care: 
"I have dealt with mental health since childhood, mostly for ADD. I stopped all that around 2018 and I'm mostly seeking care for anxiety management, I've had issues navigating generalized anxiety. I have a history of navigating panic attacks from child abuse. I was meeting with a therapist for EMDR and around the same time my husband and I also witnessed a shooting. Denver is truly out of control with violent crime. So we moved to San Francisco. Then, I also had shoulder surgery. I'm I physical therapy now to manage that, I just started returning to work full time, I was doing half days. all that has been going well. Since moving to San Francisco, now that I'm in a better place physically, I have noticed some surprises--nightmares, trouble sleeping. Not flashbacks during the day or anything, but flashback dreams. I have been taking Xanax prn as needed and even though I feel safe, I find myself in a grocery store and feel like I'm about to have a panic attack. Just having the Xanax on me was helpful in the past, but I'm finding my anxiety and panic is elevating and I'm finding I'm needing the Xanax more in the past, which was sparingly. About 3 times a week, 0.5 mg. My neurologist is prescribing the Xanax at this time, because I have migraines as well. I am having daily migraines again." 

Client's goal: "to be medication free, because I think this anxiety is situational after what we witnessed in Denver." 

Notable has therapist and getting set to do EMDR again. Also going to acupuncture. Also works with neurologist Dr. Susan Hudginson. 

On Prazosin 1 mg for nightmares, found that this was a bit too sedating for the client (interfered with morning exercise),  though did alleviate nightmares. Also taking an herbal supplement with melatonin for sleep, rx'd by neurologist. States that nightmares remain consistent--"not just shooting related, but all around trauma related. I'm hoping EMDR can help resolve that." 

Discussed other options for nightmares/sleep, as client continues to endorse nightmares, difficulty falling asleep, and difficulty staying asleep. Takes about 40 minutes to fall asleep and waking up multiple times throughout the night--"not deep or consistent, waking up between nightmares." Was on Ambien in the past for sleep, though does not wish to trial given addictive properties. Would like a "mind calming" medication to assist with sleep. States that he is "always thinking about things, anxious brain." 

We discussed gabapentin for sleep prn and discussed potential for dependency. Client verbalizes understanding of proper use and indication for this medication. Elects to trial. 

Also discussed prn, propranolol, for anxiety and "performance anxiety" and is open to trial. Discussed r/b/se and encouraged to trial instead of Xanax. 

We also discussed SSRIS for mood/anxiety/PTSD and client had a bad experience with Lexapro and declines at this time. 

Over the past several weeks, the patient endorses excessive worrying. Endorses excessive and hard-to-control worrying, feeling keyed up and on edge, difficulty concentration or mind going blank, irritability, and muscle tension. Denies panic attacks. The patient denies hopelessness, worthlessness, SI/HI/AH/VH/delusions/paranoia. 

Denies depression.very future oriented. Over the past several weeks, the patient denies any sustained sadness, hopelessness, excessive guilt or suicidal ideation. Denies anhedonia, or recent changes to appetite, energy level, or ability to concentrate. Denies any discrete periods with decreased need for sleep, euphoria or grandiosity or irritability, flights of idea, impulsivity, uncharacteristic talkativeness or rapid speech.

Notable that he is not depressed at this time, though has been voluntarily admitted x 2 in the past "for ideation and I was on too many medications." No attempts. 

Patient reports compliance to medication xanax as needed prn severe anxiety. Patient reports they are unable to function without their medication. Denies side effects or other adverse reactions to the medication. Reports appetite, sleep, energy, and concentration intact.

Denies new medical issues since last visit.

column60:
Has been doing well, feeling tired, having pain from arm and starting PT. States, "I actually feel good other than my physical condition, I was power lifting and stuff so that was good for my health." 

Notable that her sleep has much improved, sleeping through the night with Lavela capsules and Gabapentin 600 mg qhs for pain/sleep. Would like to continue this and finds the Gabapentin is helpful for anxiety, "I find it very helpful, I feel I'm way more even and even with my husband not getting as riled up." 

Has been sober from alcohol for "less than a month." Longest period of sobriety was 15-16 months. Had major win by having non alcoholic beer during football game yesterday. States that there have been triggers, but she has been able to use coping skills to get through the triggers. Regarding cravings, client continues to take Naltrexone daily and finds helpful. Tolerating well, no nausea with evening dose. Would like to continue. 

Has been on Zoloft x about 2 weeks, on 1 full tablet at this time. Tolerating well; not noticing any nausea or headache. Efficacy pending for anxiety. 

The patient denies hopelessness, worthlessness, SI/HI/AH/VH/delusions. Denies any discrete periods with decreased need for sleep, euphoria or grandiosity or irritability, flights of idea, impulsivity, uncharacteristic talkativeness or rapid speech.

Over the past several weeks, the patient ends that her excessive worry is improving. Finds that not drinking, gabapentin, have been helpful so far. 

During last session, client shared re: her anxiety,  "it's debilitating me because I want to drink...so what I'm doing now is trying to learn a new language, meditation, cleaning my house, reading and working out." Endorsed excessive and hard-to-control worrying, feeling keyed up and on edge, difficulty concentration or mind going blank, irritability, and muscle tension. However, feels they have improved since last session. Denies panic attacks. 

Denies si/hi/avh/pi. Denies mania.
_________________________________________________________________________________________________________


Initial Intake Session: 
Has been to rehab x 2, therapy on and off. She states that her chief complaint is desire to quit drinking. She states that she has been successful for periods of time, though has struggled more recently with drinking, especially with ongoing stress/anxiety/and pain. She is anticipating struggles with remaining sober with upcoming retirement (next Spring), which is huge change for her and her husband. 

She states that red blood cell count, cholesterol, were dysregulated with last blood drawn. PCP is managing this, and also put in referral for blood specialist, who said sobriety in the next 3 months is very important. Client states that last drink was bout 3-4 days ago and has been taking Naltrexone "which is very helpful for craving." She states that she is not a daily drinking, "more of binge drinker." She tolerates Naltrexone very well, has had Vivitrol injections in the past. She states that her goal is to stay sober long term. 

Notable she also has scoliosis, pain from the same. Broke hip and femur a few years ago, multiple surgeries. Starting regular physical therapy now. Exercises regularly, though encouraged to take it as easy as she can to prevent injury. Sleep is impacted by pain. Takes gabapentin (100 mg)  "every now and then, which helps." Also takes trazodone (50 mg)  irregularly. 

She endorses anxiety recently, "all the changes and my husband and I aren't on the same page about a few things, and he has a strong personality, so it's been problematic. I drink because of stress and anxiety and my husband is a big cause of it. He yells in the morning, slams doors, and it can be really hard to take it when he takes it out on me." Denies any physical abuse in the home. Denies panic attacks. She feels that alcohol helps her, "not care as much, not let things bother me, or I just yell at my husband. Otherwise, I keep things in." She adds husband brings up things from the past, which she feels is frustrating and unfair. They completed couples therapy in the past, but found it unhelpful. Feels that she could benefit from therapy on her own. 

Over the past several weeks, the patient denies sustained sadness, "Though I sometimes feel helpless in my relationship, like I don't want to be living like this in my 60s and 70s, I want to be just chill. Not all crazy." Was just in Boulder with daughter and husband was very demanding backseat driver, controlling. Denies anhedonia, recent changes to appetite, energy level, and ability to concentrate. 

Notes past memories of paternal grandfather sneaking into my room various times. She suspects the possibility of abuse, possible grooming, as father was abused by him. 

Volunteers and does haircuts for homeless people and at pig farm. Able to do work successfully, flipping house and primary caregiver for mother who has stage IV cancer. Father died of cancer a few years ago. Also watches eldest grandchild, who has nonverbal autism, once weekly. Note that she had traumatic incident where daughter died and came back to life after period in ICU "and she's not the same and her husband is not helpful and it's very hard to be around him." 

Does not have therapist at this time; recommend she start soon, though holding off for now.

column60:
Resuming care, had been seeing NP Emily Samuel while this provider was one maternity leave. 

Client presents on time to appointment. Client states that she is doing well emotionally, increasing frequency of sessions with therapist. She started weaning off of Lexapro and Abilify since last working with this provider and tolerated this reduction/cessation well emotionally, apart from some instances of frustration which felt 'a bit more extreme than in the past.' She had a few physical withdrawal symptoms, including restless leg and 'Brian zaps' though has not experienced these symptoms in about 2 months. 

She feels that Adderall and therapy once weekly  are sufficient in mitigating feeling of anxiety and low mood. Therapy is actually on maternity leave at this time, though was put with another therapist temporarily. She states, "I feel PMDD will always kick in, but I feel that the tools now make it so I can get through it." She states, "right now I feel the best I have in several years." 

Would like to discontinue propranolol prn, s uses very rarely  for work and presentations, "generalized anxiety is gone." Sleep has also improved, not needing hydroxyzine prn for sleep and would like to discontinue.

column59: I feel like my depression is coming back and that my current medication isn't doing anything. I have lack of motivation and energy.
column60:
Assumption of care. Reviewed chart and then discussed presenting problems with client. She states that she originally sought support from Mindtree to target 'mild' depression that was identified during therapy. She had been on Wellbutrin "forever" (7 or 8 years) though recently stopped (July 2025) the Wellbutrin and noticed that she was "steady off of the Wellbutrin, not even irritable." She also stopped taking Prozac since her last appointment (was on 20 mg for about 1 month, 10 mg about 2 months prior to that). Continues to take Vyvanse occasionally, though "only works a few hours." Reports she was dx with ADHD while at Mindtree, DIVA questionnaire. 

Since d/c of antidepressants July 2025, client does not notice any decompensation in mood--"the depression is better, I haven't had any dark thoughts, but I've had those thoughts in the past. I've never gotten to the point where I can't get out of bed or function." She states that she has no history of suicide attempts or self harm--"I wouldn't do that because I do not like pain, and when it would occur it would be fleeting." Has not had dark thoughts in recent memory--"not wishing I was gone, more like morbid funeral thoughts." Finds that these thoughts are not distressing when they occur. 

She still feels that her motivation is not optimal, but "better than it was before," as evidenced by difficulty working on portfolio. Low motivation is chief complaint. Denies depression. Over the past several weeks, the patient Denies any sustained sadness, hopelessness, excessive guilt or suicidal ideation. Denies anhedonia, or recent changes to appetite, energy level, or ability to concentrate. Denies any discrete periods with decreased need for sleep, euphoria or grandiosity or irritability, flights of idea, impulsivity, uncharacteristic talkativeness or rapid speech.

Endorses anxiety at times. Over the past several weeks, the patient denies any excessive worrying. Denies excessive and hard-to-control worrying, feeling keyed up and on edge, difficulty concentration or mind going blank, irritability, muscle tension, or panic attacks.

She states that her goal is to take as little medicine as possible. She feels that therapy is protective at this time, though client has not been off of antidepressants in years and will reach out of symptoms reoccur. 

Chief Complaint/goals: motivation; she discussed that Vyvanse makes her anxious and would like to hold off on adjustment of this medication. She would like to stay off of Wellbutrin (has been on this for years and has felt steady off of it) and Prozac (no efficacy noted) for now. She notices that her sleep is better off of the Wellbutrin. Will consider Pristiq in the future to target mood and motivation. 

Denies si/hi/avh/pi. Denies self harm.

Work History/Current occupation:  art teacher 
Do you enjoy your work: yes
Social support: husband and family
Current hobbies/leisure activities: "I haven't had much time for hobbies or activities outside of binge TV and reading."
Current Stressors:  "I have a twin sister that I've always tried to differentiate from, but my mother has always tried to make us be a unit, and Ive struggled a lot with defending my right to be an individual. Parents are supportive, especially my father, though my mother less so if my choices may impact my sister in some way". " Getting married this past June was a major stressor on our family dynamic, and on me. I moved across the country, got a new job, left my friends, and we haven't found a new place to live.yet, so we're squeezed in somewhere too small for me to unpack my boxes."
Legal Hx: denies
Trauma Hx: "When I was 11-13 I experienced molestation from an older neighbor, at 16 was pressured into a sexual relationship with an older boyfriend, and again before I turned 18. Saying no was hard before I reached mid twenties, and I deeply fear (perceived) sexual rejection. As a result my relationship with my new husband has been stressful as well in this area. We waited to be married to have sex,  and he has no experience outside of me, so it's been a struggle to temper what I've come to expect with what he just doesn't know or hasn't experienced. In addition to that, last year I was forcefully evicted by a landlord desperate to reno my apartment. They barraged us with papers and delivered them leaning on our doorbell early in the morning. I have been waking up to the sound of that buzzer ringing in my dreams since last fall. In the course of my move, I also experienced the loss of an entire box of my personal jewelry including pieces passed down to me by my mother and other female relatives, and have taken the loss quite hard. I spent an entire day bawling literally crying out loud about it. I'm not generally materialistic but it felt like losing myself, as much of the jewelry was gathered along the journey to becoming who I am now, from my ancestors to my bat mitzvah to my travels. It's been extremely difficult and there seems to be no resolution".

column60:
Pt is a 24 yo female, with hx MDD, GAD, and ADHD, currently on Zoloft 50 qd 

Assuming care with this provider and reviewed history of mental illness. She states that she has always struggled with anxiety, which worsened over COVID. Noticed that depression also began when COVID first started, which she feels was significantly impacted by need to leave college and return home due to lockdowns. Has been seeing Emily Samuel NP since 2020. Has been medicated on Zoloft since 2021. Did not tolerate Citalopram "it was a very very dark time in my life, very bad." 

She states-"I'm a lot better now, in remission, but I'm starting to feel some things creeping in. I'm in a very toxic job, in the process of looking for a new job, so huge life change there. I'm 24, navigating life, chronic stress maybe. Constantly on the go and I don't think I give myself time to deal with what is on my mind." She states that she has been in and out of tele heath and personal therapy since COVID as well.

column59: I am looking for a new psychiatrist, since my previous psychiatrist left the practice. Also, my current medication does not seem to be effective with depressive symptoms.
column60:
States, "I have been dealing with depression for a number of years now, around when the pandemic started. I have been taking Zoloft for about 3 years and that has helped with anxiety and symptoms of PTSD; but hasn't helped with depression. Heaviness and low energy all the time. I tried Wellbutrin, but it made me wired anxious (June 2023-beginning of 2024). So I just tried Abilify and that wasn't good." 

Started Abilify 5 mg about 2 weeks ago; started Abilify 10 mg on August 19th. Notable that did not experience side effects on Abilify 5mg, though did not any efficacy. Noticed "anxious irritability" on Abilify 10 mg, and "flu symptoms, restlessness/akathisia, achiness, and vision a bit blurry." Has not been on Abilify since after Friday's dose; flu symptoms and blurry vision have resolved since discontinuation of medication. Note that she did notice some restlessness/akathisia on the Abilify 5 mg dose "a bit, but not as bad." 

Med: 
Wellbutrin"more anxious, lots of rumination, easily annoyed, more irritable." Was on 150 mg Zoloft at the time, which is current. Notable client has been on 200 mg Zoloft two weeks ago, but went back down to 150 mg about 6 days ago, and still experiencing some sweating. 

Over the past several weeks, the patient endorses sustained sadness, "heaviness, tiredness, and sleepiness....like I work from home and have to take a nap every day." Tiredness has not impacted fulfilling obligations, though has made it very difficulty. Endorses difficulty brushing teeth/getting dressed; takes significant effort to complete self care. Endorses existential hopelessness related to the world. Denies thoughts of self harm or sib. Has never attempted suicide per pt report, nor engaged in SIB. Endorses "good network of friends I can reach out to." Has therapist, sees weekly, has been seeing therapist since March. Talk therapy, trauma work and family systems. 

Endorses anhedonia, "that's a big one, I don't really feel joy anymore." 

Without Zoloft, client was enduring "flashbacks and nightmares or things that have happened. Now, I don't experience that anymore." 

Denies  SI/HI/AH/VH/delusions. Denies any discrete periods with decreased need for sleep, euphoria or grandiosity or irritability, flights of idea, impulsivity, uncharacteristic talkativeness or rapid speech.

Anxiety, "well managed with the Zoloft." Over the past several weeks, the patient denies excessive worrying. Denies excessive and hard-to-control worrying, feeling keyed up and on edge, difficulty concentration or mind going blank, irritability, and muscle tension. Denies panic attacks.

Sleeps 8-9 hours nightly. Denies difficulty with sleep onset. 

Last labwork completed about 1 months ago. Everything was healthy and normal. Making 2,000 IU Vitamin D daily, though did not get levels. Also taking B12 daily. Denies any chronic illnesses, surgery. No seizure history, cardiac history, or asthma. 

From intake/past history: 
I have experienced anxiety throughout my whole life and depression at various times. Beginning in July 2020 to now, I have pretty consistently been experiencing symptoms of depression in the form of low energy, dread, inability to feel joy, and lack of motivation. In 2021/22 I started feeling increased anxiety and had frequent flashbacks and nightmares about times when my dad was physically violent with me as both a kid and adult. This was greatly impacting my ability to focus on work. For this I sought psychotherapy treatment. I was only able to find a psychiatrist at the time and was prescribed Zoloft in February 2022, which has helped greatly with anxiety and the flashbacks. The Zoloft helped a little bit with the depression and lifted some weight, which in turn allowed me to start leaving the house and spend less time in bed. I am still left with much of the fatigue and low mood/motivation. I saw a CBT therapist from March 2022 until September 2022 (when the therapist left the practice). I was able to work with this therapist on healthy communication and coping with anxiety around covid, but ultimately I don't think CBT was very helpful for dealing with my family issues or depression. From June 2023 to March 2024 I tried Wellbutrin to help with the depressive symptoms, but this ultimately made me feel very anxious/on edge and interfered with my ability to run, which is important to me, and so I stopped this medication. In September 2024, I was feeling increasingly fatigued, including falling asleep during work regularly and having trouble concentrating. I was prescribed a very low dosage of lamotrigine to help lift some of the heaviness. I really can't tell if this medication has any positive impact, but there are no negative side effects. I also started seeing my current therapist in March 2025 for the depressive reasons. Talk therapy has been helpful for better understanding my family system and my negative internal dialogs. Also, this therapist brought to my attention that my medication may not be working as well as it is supposed to. Regular exercise and making art have also been helpful coping techniques throughout my life, but never quite enough. I still don't want to do anything most days and don't have any forward looking hopes or aspirations. I feel very stuck and dissatisfied with life.

column60:

seen 1 week ago for refills for trazodone prn insomnia "the last prescription lasted me about a year" 

Pt returns today "I haven't been able to sleep the past few days. It's been tough. We are trying to get pregnant and I don't know if this is because of the hormones or what, have been doing acupunture treatments, i just had one and since then i've been anxious, having trouble sleeping. I threw up last saturday. I've been feeling nausous. Sometimes when i take the trazodone and if i don't fall asleep right away and power through i have nausea." 
Reports "not sure if trazodone is working as well as it used to. I get into these spirals where in the past i've been anxious and nervous that i can't sleep so i take the trazodone for a few nights and then i'm back to normal and feel balanced. I've avoided meds for most of my life and if i can sleep, my anxiety is under control. Its directly related to being able to sleep."
Reports "I have 6 year old daughter, things are stable with husband, we go to couples counseling. I also have my own therapist-"

Discussed that she may be experienced exacerbated anxiety and insomnia, as well as side effects or issues secondary to very recent acupuncture treatments, and/or a stomach bug as these symtpoms of nausea seem new and pt has history of using trazodone without side effects or nausea, with effectiveness. 
Discussed potentially trialing zoloft to target anxiety at subsequent visits if anxiety persists. Pt elects to continue to use trazodone prn insomnia, and states "maybe i'll just try OTC benadryl/melatonin/chamomile if that doesn't work." No medication adjustments made today. F/U 2 days with Grace Psych NP, who will be back from mat leave and re-establish care.

column66:
Born and raised in Pleasant Hill, CA. History of physical trauma, father was physically abusive at times, was always in a bad mood, alcoholic. Also had physical abuse from another family member (male), but only a single instance. I have a fear there is more that my family has not told me. I am not comfortable around men because of this. After parents divorced when i was 10, I was basically ignored and was barely provided for by my mother. Was constantly moving around as a child and young adult (moving to Oregon and back during high school), spending periods of time at my grandmothers (with mother who could not always maintain a home).

I found food to be a comfort, so I have some excessive eating patterns. I use to have extreme binge nights of excessive amounts of food, and would not eat again for 24 hours. This does not happen today as I have a partner around and I don't want her seeing this behavior. Today, I will still find myself eating more than I should, but not in the amounts that I used to. 

Barely finished high school, had trouble keeping grades up, despite being talented in music and sports. Did some community college but struggled with general ed classes and focused on music and graphic design instead. Left after 4 years and no degree. Worked for a little, then joined an art school for graphic design, but dropped out after 2 years. Started and grew a career in graphic design, but had challenges with demanding employers and managers who thought of me as inconsistent, lacking interest, and low energy. Eventually I left my last graphic design job in 2019, and joined a web developer bootcamp to learn web development and software engineering.

Currently work in tech as a product designer and software engineer, which I am pretty happy with today. My current employer is much more calm, controlled, and has allowed me to have a better work/life balance. I have a partner of 6 years, and will be marrying her in October. I do not have many friends aside from a couple people who I have kept in touch with at previous employers who had similar experiences, but my partner has strong connections with many people, and it has made it easier for me to get to know these people and call them my friends as well.

My current stressors are broad - I have a low tolerance for ignorant people, and people with a lack of self-awareness, so anytime I go out in publish I usually get annoyed unless I make concerted efforts to ignore everything around me. I can feel overwhelmed with all the things you need to do in the day, big and small. Work can contribute to my stress, I have anxiety whenever I need to meet my boss, despite me being aware he is the most chill and relaxed boss I've ever had. My relationship with my partner is very important to me, so I will often find myself doing many unnecessary things to keep my partner happy, which is pressure I put on myself. Social events are supposed to be relaxing or fun, but I find them stressful as I feel I need to work very hard to listen and stay engaged, and I get completely drained by the effort and unwilling to do anything else.

No legal history, aside from a divorce.

States that dating is "the only thing that brings about anxiety, I'm generally not an anxious person anymore." States that anxiety is impacting sleep, thus impacting concentration--"I was up till 3:30 am and unable to go back to bed. Just woke up anxious, and tossed and turned for the next 3 hours."

Subjective: 
"Things have been up and down, but right now very up. I'm still taking all the medication daily, no cannabis or anything, and I think the Lexapro is probably starting to help. My mood feels generally a bit more stable, I feel more in control of being able to deal with things in general. My sleep, however, is still poor. I wake up to go to the restroom and can't fall back to sleep." 

Updates: 
-Feels that Lexparo has only been protective at 15 mg/day, which was recent increase in dose. Feels mood is more stable in general, feels more in control  or emotions and able to deal with interpersonal stressors that come up. Less ruminative.  Denies side effects at this time, though has noticed some night sweats, non distressing. Will continue to monitor. Elects to continue this medication.

-Feels the propranolol has been helping prior to going on dates. Tolerating well, no side effects. Elects to continue this medication--"it's been hugely helpful." Also utilizing gabapentin and feels this is very helpful. 

-Denies depression today. Denies persistent hopelessness, helplessness, worthlessness. Meeting with therapist tomorrow. Denies suicidal thoughts. Denies urges to self harm. 

-Feels that relationship with Haley has been going well, feels that they had a conversation that was very clarifying for him  the other day, which offered relief. 

-Sleep continues to be interrupted, difficulty falling back to sleep after getting up to urinate--"I just wake up at 3 am and start overthinking." Encouraged to discussed with therapist. Discussed adjustment of Trazodone dose, addition of middle of night dose of gabapentin, or trial of another medication. Client elects to trial gabapentin for sleep. 

-Though continued therapy is indicated, continued psychopharmacological intervention is also warranted, especially given history of severe and debilitating anxiety, which had reemerged prior to seeing this provider; now, seems to be more manageable. 

-Has never been dx with ADHD. Has never been dx with OCD. Does ruminate over dating, obsess over dating, "I'll send a text and reread it and wonder if what I said was stupid, recheck it, I don't typically have that kind of attachment with my phone." This may be better attributed to anxiety than OCD. Will continue to monitor. Client feels that this has improved somewhat recently, with increased dose of Lexapro, and uses of prns. 

-Denies any discrete periods with decreased need for sleep, euphoria or grandiosity or irritability, flights of idea, impulsivity, uncharacteristic talkativeness or rapid speech.

-Currently taking medication for hair regrowth and triglyceride management. 

-Appetite has improved, as anxiety is better managed. 

-States that he is hoping to do more intentional exercise (hike, lifting). Discussed this is a very good intervention for anxiety. 

Denies si/hi/vh/pi. Denies mania.

She states that she is working on getting to know more people in the city--"not many people besides my roommate." Notable that she is close with family ad childhood friend who live only an hour away. Identifies them as support system. 

Has had a very stressful period of work recently, handing it better than she would have been in the past, per pt report. She sees this as positive change.

She is no longer a participant in a  study at UCSF where she is cutting out ultra-processed foods, as she felt that maybe it wasn't the best for her to participate in a purposefully restrictive diet. This demonstrated good insight. 

States that about 2 years ago, she was in "a bad place and not eating a lot every day and then I'd have some episodes at night of bingeing and purging." States that she has been in grossly remission from these symptoms since May of 2023, having engaged in these behaviors "just once or twice Fall 2023." Has not engaged since. State that she will reach out to this provider if this becomes a problem again. She has found that working on eating habits with therapist had been very helpful. She is unsure if she would like to reestablish with a therapist. 

States that she identifies with the dx of dysthymia. States that 'warning signs' of more severe depression is isolation, 'going off the grid, like not going to social things, not replying to my friends."  States that she is not isolating at this time, "I've been making an active effort to not do that, but sometimes it feels very tiring and overwhelming." Intentionally calling friends instead of texting them, "which feels less overwhelming." States that she felt good during her recent birthday week, as friends and family were reaching out frequently and expressed care. 

Sleep is "fine, about 7-8 hours nightly."

States that this has become manageable and rare since starting therapy 3 years ago. Still experienced some hyper-vigilance and "feeling unsafe." Discussed prn propranolol for the same.

column62:
I began seeing a therapist in January 2021 due to intrusive thoughts from sexual trauma when I was 16 (around 2018). I was diagnosed with GAD, SAD, and MDD (moderate) around May 2021. I began seeing a psychiatrist in March 2022 to help symptoms of anxiety and depression.

I regularly saw my therapist once a week or once every other week, and I saw my psychiatrist about every 3-4 weeks throughout college (i.e., until May 2024). I saw my therapist and psychiatrist for the last time in August 2024. I have since not seen a mental health professional.

I started reliving and becoming fixated on that experience and developed depressive symptoms in the fall of 2020. I began seeing a therapist in January 2021, and I was diagnosed with GAD, SAD, and MDD (moderate) around May 2021. In the 2021-2022 school year, I began experiencing extreme irritation from certain sounds that would lead to heightened states of anxiety and, on a few occasions, would escalate to panic attacks. I began seeing a psychiatrist and was prescribed Lexapro in March 2022. In May 2022, I underwent a neuropsychological examination that led to my diagnosis of ADHD (moderate, combined type). Around July 2022, I started on Vyvanse to help manage ADHD.

For the past several years, I have noticed that I experience ~2-week cycles of depression, and my therapist suggested that my diagnosis of MDD might be better fit as persistent depressive disorder (PDD). Though medication helps, I struggle with emotional dysregulation, which is common among people with ADHD. Because of my past trauma, I have extreme distrust and fear of men.

States "things have been going pretty well. Taking the Dexedrine as prescribed and the Trazodone and tolerating well." States that mood has been "generally okay, it fluctuates...some mild depression. John reports that the dexedrine is working well for him. He is currently using trazodone to aid with sleep, noting that some weeks he uses it more and some weeks less. column60:
Client presents to appointment on time. Reviewed intake information. Had first appointment with therapist who she feels is good fit. Client started all medications as prescribed. To take full tablet of Zoloft today, has tolerated it well, taking with Zofran and hasn't experienced any nausea thus far. Discussed that Zoloft takes 4-6 weeks to see efficacy, "though placebo effect, I feel some empowerment, so that's helping." Not noticing any symptoms indicative of manic episode (no euphoria, grandiosity, or sleeplessness). 

Sleep has improved somewhat this week, which she contributes to drinking cessation and "creating a bit more of a purposeful routine at night, knowing that I'll take the gabapentin." She feels that the gabapentin 100 mg helped her feel calmer at bedtime. Some grogginess noted in the morning, though shifted to taking it easier in the evening and tolerated better. Would like to continue this regimen for sleep--"it turns down the volume on things that would otherwise keep me up." Encouraged to trial higher dose of the same, if indicated (rx'd 100 mg to 300mg). 

Client also trialed propranolol prn and felt it "takes the edge off without somnolence." However, felt a little uncomfortable after taking it for a few days. Has been taking 1 propranolol in the morning to "get through the morning pressure." We reviewed her asthma diagnosis, "it's not severe anymore, I've grown out of it. It's just triggered if I'm at a house with cats or have bronchitis. It's very rare." She states, "I can't remember the last time I used my inhaler and I'm not around any of those triggers." Client verbalized understanding of risk related to propranolol and albuterol inhaler working less well and client verbalizes understanding of risk and elects to continue. Denies any lightheadedness with propranolol, tolerating well. 

She feels the prns are helping her refrain from use of alcohol, "I only need one, I feel like I have better tools." 

Over the past several weeks, the patient endorses sustained sadness. Endorses anhedonia, recent changes to appetite, energy level, and ability to concentrate.

The patient endorses persistent hopelessness, worthlessness, purposelessness and loss of joy, interest, or value in things she used to enjoy, "things feel very pointless." Lowered motivation, "I stayed in bed until 1pm last weekend because I thought what's the point of getting up?" Notes that completing ADLs is more difficult. 

Denies SI/HI/AH/VH/delusions. 


From intake/presenting issues:
States, "I think it's been a long time coming that I've needed to see someone. I just married my husband in June and he's the best thing in my life. I'm looking to freeze my eggs because of some of my gut issues. They recommended a carrier. It's the process of starting a family that I am encountering hormone changes. I have been on a low dose birth control for many years and having to stop that and have new mood swings is really triggering. I'm just sad all day since June. I didn't realize how triggering it would be for me experiencing some depressive symptoms that I've had in the past. It's hard to find joy in normal things and my anxiety is through the roof right now, like clenching and unable to relax or sleep. A vicious cycle. To cope with everything, I have been drinking in a way that's out of control. Drinking to a level where I'll be so hungover the next day that I'll have to cancel a work appointment." 

She is anticipating having to be off of birth control (which she finds had been regulating for mood over the past 25 years, Nuvaring) for awhile and has anticipatory anxiety about having to navigate feelings and hormonal fluctuations with egg retrieval. Also notes a complicated relationship with mother, which is being triggered by family planning. Recommending regular therapy in addition to possible medication management, given these stressors in the context of family planning. g, as well as fears related to motherhood. 

Notable that she has multiple food allergies and nervous system dysregulation which causes flight or flight. She has a specialist in Los Angeles managing her food allergies and digestive system challenges (Eosinophilic esophagitis, Abdominophrenic dysenergia).  Denies any discrete periods with decreased need for sleep, "I always feel exhausted."

column66:
I was born in New York City and moved to Maryland when I was in grade school. My mother came from a New York family with money and my father was from Baltimore and did not have money. I always felt I did not get along with my mother at a young age and was frustrated by her behavior. I felt trapped by her inability to get things done or function normally within social interactions despite what that meant for her kids in tow despite spoiling her absolute love and devotion to us. She thought me beauty. She is an artist as am I. 
My father traveled a lot and I tended to run our house when he was gone. I cooked a lot because of my food allergies which made it hard to eat out or go on a school field trip even. My parents fought a lot. They eventually divorced when I was in college after years of separation. 
My sister is 6 years younger and we always got along well. We still do. We sense we had a lot of trauma as kids with fighting parents and a challenging dynamic with what was considered a supportive childhood. We talk a lot about how to try and manage our relationship with our mother. We are both very close with our father but he is also a friend more than a parent, at least to me.

column60:
States "I've been treated for depression since age 12. Two years ago I had an episode of psychosis and was put on Haldol. I haven't had any psychotic episodes since then. My last provider kept things the same as they had given me in the hospital. There haven't been any changes in awhile. I'm feeling depressed now, sleeping a lot. I am looking for adjustment in my medications."

Regarding depression, client just moved from Georgia to the Bay Area a few months ago. Move prompted by husband's work. She is unemployed, looking for work, finding this challenging. She is a chef by trade. She feels isolated, no community in the Bay area. 

Client has noticed hypersomnia (sleeping for 24 hours 1-2 times a week, 'not being able to wake up, started several months ago,' 10 hours on other nights and during the daytime), weight gain (since being on Haldol x 2 years). Endorses significant difficulty completing ADLS since moving to Bay Area (difficulty showing, getting dressed, brushing teeth...notable that client has not brushed her teeth x 1 week). She denies changes in energy levels, concentration, appetite. 

Over the past several weeks, the patient endorses periods of sadness throughout the day, crying easily, lowered tolerance for distress. Notable that she has not noticed any irritability, though has struggled with this in the past. Endorses anhedonia. 

Primary pain point is hypersomnia--"I;m bored with nothing to do, so no motivation behind getting up. And I'm tired because of my fibromyalgia." 

Regarding tiredness, client has not seen a PCP x 1 year. Labs drawn at that time and client reports everything was healthy and normal. She is looking for a primary care physician to manage this disease. 

Uses OTC ibuprofen and cannabis 20-40 mg daily x 5 years. She states that she has been using cannabis for years to "help with the fibromyalgia." When asked if she is wiling to cut back on the cannabis, client states, "I can, but it makes me more reliant on other medications, like anti-inflammatories and I'd have to go back on Lyrica." Notable that client was using 20-40 mg cannabis daily during the tine that she experiences psychosis. 

The patient endorses "a little bit" of hopelessness, "a little bit" of worthlessness, denies SI/HI/AH/VH/delusions. 

Regarding suicidal ideation, client has struggled with si in the past, "passive thoughts during the past two years, but nothing specific no plan." Denies si currently, no intent or plan. Attempted suicide x 1 as a teenager and was hospitalized x 1 about 2 years ago due to intrusive thoughts about "driving my car off the road." Denies SIB at this time --"sometimes I hit myself, but not to the point of brushing or injury. Last happened two weeks ago, hitting myself with an open fist...triggered by stress, moving, normal life stuff."  

Denies any discrete periods with decreased need for sleep, euphoria or grandiosity or irritability, flights of idea, impulsivity, uncharacteristic talkativeness or rapid speech. Denies these symptoms during episode 2 years ago. 

Over the past several weeks, the patient endorses "some" anxiety, "but the hydroxyzine 25 mg tid helps." Client has been on this medication x 3-4 years. She also finds that meditation is helpful and "curling up with my weighted blanket." Denies panic attacks recently, though has struggled with this in the past. Client is satisfied with where her anxiety is at this time, well controlled. 

Regarding PTSD symptoms, "I was attacked a few years ago and I have had PTSD symptoms since...flashbacks, triggers, panic attacks. I haven't had a panic attack in awhile, which was great. My medications have helped, for sure." 

Client states that she struggles with obsessive, repetitive thoughts, mostly related to safety "some paranoia." Gives example, "I have had repeated thoughts that I am being listened to through my devices just at home." She feels that this paranoia is manageable with behavioral interventions, not impacting her every day life, and does not feel it needs to be addressed at this time. Will continue to monitor. 

She states that she has struggled with these thoughts consistently, over the past few years, since hospitalizations. Denies a/vh, denies any thoughts of harming self or others.

Client states that she feels she is able to stop the cannabis as this provider does not feel comfortable managing medications to someone with history of psychosis and concurrent cannabis use. Discussed partial hospitalization programs to address cannabis use, depression, and client states that she is not interested in this, but can stop the cannabis on her own. Will continue to monitor.

Discussed that there may be law of diminishing returns with increase in Pristiq, though client elects to trial higher dose, as has benefitted from dose increase in the past. She wishes to continue Haldol, as she feels this has kept psychotic symptoms at bay, though has some persistent and residual paranoia related to "devices" monitoring her. She denies any diagnosis of psychotic disorder, thought this provider requests that she send chart from previous provider if able. Does not appear to be responding to internal stimuli, though affect a bit odd. She uses CBT skills to cope with these thoughts, acknowledging that they are not grounded in reality. Encouraged client to follow up with PCP to discuss options for weight management on Haldol and get yearly physical given history of fibromyalgia. This provider encouraged client to stop use of cannabis (20-40 mg/day reported) given psychotic history and client states that she is able to do this without support from outpatient program. this provider made clear that she will have to refer client elsewhere if she is unable to stop using cannabis. Presents from the beach, just moved to Pacific Beach on Friday. Finds that it has been a good move thus far, 'I'm happy here.' 

States that she has finished bottle of Strattera. States that she is 'unsure if I want to be dependent on it.' States that she felt it was helpful, felt more focused, 'but I don't know if it was just me getting on my game or if it's the prescription.' States that she has had therapy x 2 sessions, has another session this week as well. States that therapy has been helpful, 'getting my feelings out has been so good.' 

States that she is very much convinced of her ADHD diagnoses, 'though I think I can cure it myself.' States that she would like to work on therapy first, 'managing my feelings.' However, would be open to trying one more month of Strattera at higher dose to see if she notices more of a benefit, as her sx continue to interfere with every day life. Discussed r/b/se of the same. 

Completed DIVA questionnaire prior to session. Scored high for inattention as well as hyperactivity, though predominantly inattentive. States that she put off completing this form, forgot to put her groceries away, 'and my room is a mess.' Endorses the following symptoms of ADHD: difficulty with sustained concentration, difficulty finishing tasks, easily side tracked, difficulty prioritizing tasks, lose track of thoughts, lose/misplace things easily, difficulty sitting still, constant fidgeting. Would like to complete more formal assessment of the same. DIVA assessment positive for ADHD. 

Also discussed antidepressants, as client met criteria for depression at first session together. Client declines at this time. Denies feelings of hopelessness and worthlessness. Denies suicidal thoughts. Denies urges to self harm.  Endorses anhedonia. State that she is ofen 'eating too much, constantly feeling hungry.' Denies bingeing, purging, or restricting. Completed ADLs. 

Has not been formally medicated or ADHD in the past. Has, however, taken ADHD stimulants in the past.  States, 'there's one ADHD medication I took that made me feel super slow, sitting around, a zombie.' Open to trying non-sitmulant ADHD medication. States, 'I am worried that it could interfere with my health, make me feel like a zombie or lose my appetite.' 

Denies excessive worrying, Denies panic attacks. 

States she feels safe at home, no access to weapons. Living with new roommates. States she has been sober from cannabis x  8 months. States that she hasn't seen her roommate use cannabis. 

Sleep has been 'good, 8 or 9 hours.' Got alarm clock so she can be on more of a consistent schedule, waking up and surfing in the morning as ultimate goal for client. 

Denies any discrete periods with decreased need for sleep, euphoria or grandiosity or irritability, flights of idea, impulsivity, uncharacteristic talkativeness or rapid speech.

column59: My therapist recommended it
column60:
"I have two kids, ages 4 and 3, and I'm a special education teacher. I felt like I was getting very anxious, lashing out at my kids, being super irritable at the end of the day. I just wanted some help, so I reached out to a therapist. I scheduled a therapy appointment and the week before the appointment I got into a collision where my car flipped, but we walked away unscathed. But after that, I got pink eye, then a staff infection in my face, and then a friend of mine recently passed away about a month ago. All of these things have just kept building and building." States that her therapist recommended that she see a psych provider do discuss medications, wanting to explore options.

States, "If things were different, I'd like to not be set off by my kids crying. I use up all my patience throughout the day on my students and when I get home my patience is empty. My kids don't get the best version of me." Would like to be more calm/relaxed. Would like a higher tolerance for distress/more stamina/more reserve left for children.

Denies depression at this time, "I feel like I've felt good over the past couple of weeks, but then there are times I feel very down. I semi to come in waves. I definitely feel I had very bad post party depression after my son was born, and that hit really hard, didn't reach out for help and get through it on my own. That was a good year of just really feeling down." Support system at home consists of husband, a few friends, therapist.  Also supported by in laws and sister in law who lives up the street, mom also helps with her children 2 days a week. 

Over the past several weeks, the patient denies any sustained sadness, hopelessness, excessive guilt or suicidal ideation. Notable that she experienced some passive suicidal ideations in the past, "when I was younger, like passive thoughts of I wonder what it would be like to drive off the road, but never acted on it." Denies history of self harm.

Very busy, feels that she has to be very intentional to enjoy quality time with her children because her free time is so little. Endorses tightness in her chest, feeling tense overall, feeling more reactive recently. Feeling overwhelmed about meeting deadlines, work, family obligations--"I have so much to do that I can't even start, it's just overwhelming." 

Endorses excessive worry, mostly related to work. Endorses intrusive thoughts about driving recently, especially after car accident, worries about catastrophic things happening "like my kids dying" since having son. States, "I'd have some of these irrational fears, like if I leave the room my daughter will step on his head and hurt him, or they will break their neck on the couch, things like that." States that she tries to use cognitive interventions and distractor to help her cope with these thoughts. Also staying off of social media, "I'd get into these debit holes of horrible stories and things happening, so I try to stay away from things on tik tok that make me anxious." 

Feels that her worry has not been impacting sleep (sleeping 7-8 hours/night), "because the thoughts are pretty fleeting, I don't feel like I get stuck on them or that it causes any physical reaction...but more-so makes me feel mentally distressed." States that her worries and intrusions take a lot of her mental energy, especially when she has downtime, or when things are quiet. 

States that her husband has noticed that she has been having a more challenging time managing her emotions and is easier to anger/more irritable since the car accidents last month. Also supported her though the postpartum depression. Husband is very supportive of her getting help. 

Discussed OCD and no compulsions associated with her intrusions/worries. Does not meet criteria for OCD at this time. 

Denies panic attacks. 

Denies any discrete periods with decreased need for sleep, euphoria or grandiosity or irritability, flights of idea, impulsivity, uncharacteristic talkativeness or rapid speech.

Would be due for PCP appointment, last appointment was 1 year ago. Would like to get lab work completed and EKG, given some chest discomfort. 

Endorses nightmares about people dying, but happens infrequently. Will continue to monitor. 

Denies si/hi/avh/pi. Denies mania.

This provider encouraged higher level of care in form of IOP, or working with outside in-person psychiatrist in addition to her weekly therapy sessions, who would be able to accommodate disability requests and monitor vitals in person as well. Client agreed that she would benefit from in person care, and this provider is sent her referrals, including Inspire Psychiatric in Carlsbad, CA and Kaizen Brain Center (please refer to email below).  

Client denied suicidal ideations or urges to self harm last session, and was gross future-oriented. Safety plan was put in place & reviewed.  

Endorsed having professional support of weekly therapy and nutritionist visits. Instructed to d/c Vyvanse as she was not tolerating it well and continued on combination of  Wellbutrin and Lexapro until she could follow up with psychiatrist. Notable that she also reported taking her medications inconsistently, which could have contributed to her reduction in mood symptoms and we discussed the importance of compliant. 

____________________________________________________________________________________________________________________

The following email was sent following the last session on 9/16: 

"Hi Adalaide, 

Just following up on our discussion. I'm excited for you to establish with someone in person. 

Typically, the psychiatrists embedded in these in-person practices are able to accommodate disability requests as well. I know this is imperative at this time for you, given your medical leave from work. I am unable to do this for you. I'd also encourage you to discuss with your PCP in the interim, as I know migraines are significantly impacting your ability to function. 

The Kaizen Brain Center would be my first choice for you, as they even have a special headache clinic that is tied to their mental healthcare. Noor Bahramzhi is lovely and I hear great things about Dr. Ahmed (she works under him). 

https://www.kaizenbraincenter.com/team
https://www.inspirepsychiatric.com
https://www.hemi.health/whatweoffer

If you were to pursue an IOP program during this month off, Wings Recovery is great (https://www.wingsrecovery.org). Worthy Wellness is newer, but may be too heavily substance-focused for you. 

Let me know if you need anything else or if you need additional referrals. 

Please call 911 if there is a psychiatric or medical emergency. 

Sincerely, 

Grace PMHNP "

-Abilify and Rexulti--"They both made me too tired, I'd sleep 15 or 16 hours at a time." Had adequate trial of both (a few months). 
-Effexor, stopped because was having side effects (migraines, sweating)

I made the appointment because I’ve been struggling with persistent anxiety, poor sleep, and difficulty focusing during the day. It’s affecting both my personal and professional life, and I’m looking for medical support to manage it.
In your own words, please provide as much background information as possible regarding the issue(s) you are seeking help with. When did you start noticing your struggles? How have they affected your life? What have you done to try and help yourself so far? What worked and what didn't work? etc.
Over the past several months, I’ve been under significant stress due to work demands and responsibilities. I constantly feel anxious and tense, especially in the evenings. My mind races at night, and I often can’t fall asleep until very late — sometimes I only get 3–4 hours of broken sleep. This leads to fatigue, irritability, and poor concentration the next day.

During the day, I find it extremely difficult to stay focused. I’m easily distracted, forget important tasks, and feel mentally scattered. It’s hurting my productivity, confidence, and ability to manage my business effectively.

I’ve tried natural solutions like cutting caffeine, using calming apps (like Headspace and Calm), magnesium supplements, and setting a strict sleep schedule, but none of that has worked. I’ve also tried taking short breaks and using task-management tools during the day, but my mind still wanders, and I feel overwhelmed.

I’m seeking help to manage both the anxiety and the inability to focus. I’ve read about medications like Xanax for anxiety and Adderall for focus and would like to explore whether those or other medical options might be appropriate for me — even short term — under proper supervision.
All current medications (psychotropic and non-psychotropic)
None regularly. Occasionally I take magnesium or melatonin, but they have not been effective.

-Continue Propranolol 10 mg; 1-2 tabs (10 mg to 20 mg) po tid prn for restlessness (do not take within 2 hours of Lisinopril. HOLD if blood pressure is less than 90 systolic, less than 60 diastolic, or HR less than 65; no dizziness with propranolol, tolerating well) 
-Continue Hydroxyzine 50 mg; 1 tab po tid prn for anxiety/sleep 
-Continue Cogentin 0.5 mg; 1 tab po qhs for restlessness (has not needed) 


States, "I feel that I've been doing really well. I've paired taking the Abilify with the propranolol. I do feel a bit of lower body restlessness, just moving my legs, but that's an improvement from where it was, like feeling like I have to move around. My sleep has improved, which is good, because with the Abilify at 5 mg I would wake up and be restless." 

We discussed r/b of being on Abilify and that 2 mg is a small dose. Discussed that the side effects may outweigh the benefit of a mood stabilizing antipsychotic at this dose. Notable that client would like to trial the Abilify for longer at this time, as he states that he feels "calmer since taking it, not as irritable honestly, but that could also be because I haven't' been taking any type of steroid." Will continue to monitor. 

Client also shares that he has been dealing with herniated disc since October and has been on oral steroids since December. We discussed now that steroids can trigger mania or depression, depending on dose. Client shares that he is worried about upcoming steroid injection on Friday, as he felt oral steroid may have contributed to dysregulation and wanting to r/o bipolar disorder. He already had discussed this with pain management doctor and pain management said it should be fine. Discussed that if he notices negative psychological reaction or safety concerns, to go to the emergency department. 

Notable that this provider has not seen Angel since June, with some gap in care due to insurance coverage. Client in the interim has gotten off of Lamotrigine (hair loss) and Latuda ("did not tolerate in any capacity) and has since experienced possible hypomania--endorsing periods with decreased need for sleep ("several days at at a time with just 2-3 hours of sleep a night and nor being tired or impacted the next day"), increased energy, increased impulsivity (spending money, excessive social outings, increase sex drive, speeding), increased talkativeness (to the point where others notice), increase in goal directed-behaviors (picking up extra tasks at work, though not always finishing), and irritability during those episodes. Last episode occurred about January 31st and ended around February 7th. Feels that this was not followed by depressive episode--"just extremely exhausted, drained, but not necessarily depressed. Like the energy that was there was completely gone." Will continue to monitor. 

Discussed options of switching to another typical antipsychotic or switching to mood stabilizing medication like lithium or Depakote. Client elects to continue Abilify at a lower dose, acknowledging this may not be protective for mood stability at 2 mg. Again, discussed this provider's recommendation is to switch but client is not currently DTO or DTS or GS and would like to trial adequate trial of Abilify 2 mg first. 

No manic symptoms noted at this time. No hyperactivity, pressured speech, or racing thoughts noted at this time. 

Denies persistent hopelessness, helplessness, worthlessness. Denies passive suicidal ideations, which is an improvement. No intent or plan. Denies urges to self harm.

Encouraged to stay in therapist if able. States, "I don't feel like I'm own a particularly low place where I can't handle thing right now." Continues to see therapist. 

Anxiety well controlled--"I take hydroxyzine very rarely if and when I need it."  Notable taking gabapentin prn, rx'd by PCP for nerve pain, 600 mg to 900 mg prn, taking infrequently. 

Denies changes in health status since last sessions. No hospitalizations or need for higher level of care since last session. 

Denies self-medicating with substances at this time, "not drinking anymore like I was. There was some problematic behaviors Dec-February and I have stopped because I noticed I wasn't drinking healthily." States that pain is slowly improving over time. Will continue to monitor. Will be seeing pain specialist later this week, with plan for epidural.
